docs.text: Welcome to MkDocs For full documentation visit mkdocs.org . Commands mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project. mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server. mkdocs build - Build the documentation site. mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit. Project layout mkdocs.yml # The configuration file. docs/ index.md # The documentation homepage. ... # Other markdown pages, images and other files.
docs.title: Home

docs.text: mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project. mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server. mkdocs build - Build the documentation site. mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.
docs.title: Commands

docs.title: Project layout

docs.text: Install MkDocs You should now have the mkdocs command installed on your system. Run 'mkdocs --version' to check that everything worked okay. $ mkdocs --version mkdocs, version 1.2.0 from /usr/local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/mkdocs (Python 3.8) :::tip If you would like manpages installed for MkDocs, the click-man tool can generate and install them for you. Simply run the following two commands: ::: pip install click-man click-man --target path/to/man/pages mkdocs See the click-man documentation for an explanation of why manpages are not automatically generated and installed by pip.
docs.title: Install

docs.text: You should now have the mkdocs command installed on your system. Run 'mkdocs --version' to check that everything worked okay. $ mkdocs --version mkdocs, version 1.2.0 from /usr/local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/mkdocs (Python 3.8) :::tip If you would like manpages installed for MkDocs, the click-man tool can generate and install them for you. Simply run the following two commands: ::: pip install click-man click-man --target path/to/man/pages mkdocs See the click-man documentation for an explanation of why manpages are not automatically generated and installed by pip.
